recipes.name: Al deposition @ high rate
recipes.desc: Suitable for depositing high conductivity aluminium thin films. Slow ramp the heating of the crucible (5 mins from 0% to 40%). Look for a rate of 2 A/s (~50%), then close the shutter and increase the voltage by 50%. Allow the crucible to heat briefly (<30 s) and for the rate to increase to >15 A/s before opening the shutter briefly (3 secs). After closing the shutter, immediately reduce the voltage and start a gradual ramp down.
recipes.tips: Each voltage source has a different optimal voltages. Deposit Al at a high rate to reduce the oxidation of the aluminium and improve the sheet conductivity of the film. Baking the chamber with a slow voltage ramp, and achieving a low chamber pressure will further minimise moisture. Be careful not to consume the aluminium material before finishing the deposition.
